返回本批次

是一品脱吗？

Is it a pint?

isitapint.com 作者 cainxinth 分数 175 评论 152 2026/03/24 00:13
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 多年来无数次倒酒让我想到了一个问题：“这真的是一品脱吗？他们这么说，但真的吗？”这些发现催生了“品脱巡逻队”，这是一项草根运动，可能会改变美国人享用啤酒、葡萄酒和苹果酒的方式——走向正义的诚实。本文记录了该运动的最初发现和发起。简介 方法 数据 了解您的玻璃杯 讨论 参与其中 应用程序下载链接 商品 给我买一杯啤酒 © 2025–2026 Steven Nilsen。版权所有。

Original Summary

  1. Years of countless pours led me to the question “Is this really a pint? They say so, but really?” The findings gave rise to the Pint Patrol, a grassroots movement that could change how Americans are served their beer, wine, and cider—toward righteous honesty. This article documents the initial discovery and the inception of the movement. INTRODUCTION METHODS DATA KNOW YOUR GLASS DISCUSSION GET INVOLVED App Download Links Here MERCH BUY ME A BEER © 2025–2026 Steven Nilsen. All rights reserved.

评论（默认中文）

jjgreen 2026/03/24 00:17
在约克郡，喝一小品脱会导致“你能在里面放一杯威士忌吗？”、“当然可以”、“然后用啤酒填充它”
In Yorkshire a short pint will result in "Could you fit a whiskey in there?", "yes of course", "THEN FILL IT WITH BEER"
在 HN 中查看
calebelac 2026/03/24 00:33
我将开始使用这种方法。干杯!
I'm going to start using this approach. Cheers!
在 HN 中查看
lucideer 2026/03/24 00:34
我来自爱尔兰，在爱尔兰，精确地倒满啤酒实际上是一种宗教，如果在你拿起刚倒的一品脱啤酒的时间内，酒头减少得太快，许多调酒师甚至会把玻璃杯收回并加满。

一直让我感到奇怪的一件事是，在像比利时这样的啤酒圣地，文化似乎与此相反——不仅玻璃杯通常要小得多（这很好），而且它们还在顶部留下了巨大的间隙。玻璃容量根本不被视为靠近边缘。

I'm from Ireland, where filling beers precisely up to the brim is practically a religion, & many barmen will even take the glass back & top it up if they see the head diminishing too quickly in the space of time it takes you to pick the freshly poured pint up.

One thing that always struck me as odd is how the culture is seemingly the opposite of this in apparent beer meccas like Belgium - not only are the glasses typically much smaller (this is fine) but they also leave massive gaps at the top. The glass capacity is never treated as being close to the rim at all.

在 HN 中查看
pmh 2026/03/24 01:02
> 眼镜不仅通常要小得多（这很好），而且在顶部也会留下巨大的间隙

我想知道这是否是由于桶装啤酒与瓶装/桶装啤酒的流行所致。前者在比利时相对不常见，你想要后者的头。

也就是说，超大的玻璃器皿（例如 Duvel 的郁金香芳香剂）和/或灌装线也用于容纳头部，同时仍然不会欺骗顾客的体积。

> not only are the glasses typically much smaller (this is fine) but they also leave massive gaps at the top

I'm wondering if this is due to the prevalence of cask ales vs bottle/keg conditioning. The former is relatively uncommon in Belgium and you want the head from the latter.

That said, oversized glassware (e.g. Duvel's tulip for aromatics) and/or fill lines are also used to accommodate the head while still not cheating the customer out of volume.

在 HN 中查看
lucideer 2026/03/24 02:27
它很可能是由于头部的流行而成为一种文化规范，但我经常看到非常温和的头部，在泡沫上方留有间隙。

> 不欺骗客户的数量

如果这是常态的话，我不认为这是作弊。人们会期望根据平均服务量适当设定价格（即满杯将是一种奖励，而不是差距是一种损失）。

我确实觉得很奇怪，因为我来自相反的文化背景。

It could well have emerged as a cultural norm from the prevalence of heads, but I've seen it often for very moderate heads, with a gap left above the foam.

> not cheating the customer out of volume

I don't think it's cheating if its the norm. One would expect prices to be set appropriately for the average volume served (i.e. a full glass would be a bonus rather than the gap being a loss).

I do just find it odd, coming myself from the opposite culturally.

在 HN 中查看
lokar 2026/03/24 06:04
我有几杯那种款式的（la chouffe），它们有 33cl 的标记，我认为是满杯的
I have a couple glasses of that style (la chouffe), they have a mark for 33cl, which I assume is a full pour
在 HN 中查看
jccooper 2026/03/24 06:10
比利时酒杯就像酒杯一样，为酒头和芳香留出了空间。

比利时也热衷于将玻璃杯与啤酒相匹配，而欧洲在玻璃杯上有非常明智的倾倒线，因此它是为一定的灌装量而设计的，并按照设计灌装。

它是为体验而不是体积而设计的。

Belgian glasses, like wine glasses, leave room for head and aromatics.

Belgium is also fanatical about matching the glass with the beer, and Europe has the very sensible pour line on glasses, so it is designed for a certain fill and filled to the design.

It's designed for experience, not volume.

在 HN 中查看
ahofmann 2026/03/24 01:00
我记得看过短号三部曲（ https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Three_Flavours_Cornetto ）并且总是想知道到底为什么他们把杯子装满。这样运输起来很不舒服，而且啤酒不会洒出来。
I remember watching the cornetto trilogy ( https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Three_Flavours_Cornetto ) and always wondering why on earth they fill up the glasses up to the brim. It is uncomfortable to transport this way and not spill beer.
在 HN 中查看
fanf2 2026/03/24 01:15
当 CAMRA 在 20 世纪 70 年代初刚刚兴起时，他们发起了一场超大尺寸玻璃杯的宣传活动，将一品脱酒杯放在杯边，而不是把一品脱酒杯放在杯沿上，这样玻璃杯中就有空间容纳一品脱液体和酒头。大型啤酒厂讨厌这个想法，并发起了一场反动运动，认为更换所有玻璃杯或为顾客提供他们所支付的全部服务会太昂贵。 （我的父亲是吉尼斯世界纪录的新员工，遗憾的是他早期的任务之一就是“杯装满溢”活动。）
When CAMRA was new in the early 1970s, they started a campaign for oversize glasses holding a pint to the line instead of a pint to the rim, so that there would be space for a pint of liquid and a head in the glass. The big breweries hated this idea and mounted a reactionary campaign arguing things like it would be too expensive to replace all the glasses, or serve customers the full measure they had paid for. (My father was a new recruit at Guinness and sadly one of his early tasks was the pint-to-brim campaign.)
在 HN 中查看
JetSetIlly 2026/03/24 02:44
我不再在酒吧喝酒，但在我附近，专门卖桶装啤酒的酒吧通常都有带衬里的玻璃杯。

问题是，许多人坚持将玻璃杯装满，因为他们觉得自己少了零钱。所以它解决了一个问题，但又带来了另一个问题。

I no longer drink in pubs but in my neck of the woods, the pubs that specialised in cask ale often had lined glasses.

The problem was that many people insisted on the glass being filled to the brim, because they felt they were being short changed. So it solved one problem but created another.

amonon 2026/03/24 01:07
我的理解是，比利时啤酒文化认为香气是体验的重要组成部分。但我从来没有去过，这都是潜移默化的。
My understanding is Belgian beer culture considers the aroma to be an important part of the experience. But I’ve never been, that’s all through osmosis.
在 HN 中查看
dkarl 2026/03/24 02:33
就是这样。这就是为什么玻璃杯具有与酒杯和小酒杯相同的基本形状（杯柄、碗和锥形边缘）。液体位于碗中，香气被液体和边缘之间的空间捕获。
This is exactly it. That's why the glasses have the same basic form (stem, bowl, and tapered rim) as wine glasses and snifters. The liquid sits in the bowl, and the aroma is captured in the empty space between the liquid and the rim.
在 HN 中查看
dismalaf 2026/03/24 00:55
在捷克共和国，他们通常倒三指头。 但测量线也位于玻璃下方，泡沫全部位于该线上方......
In Czech Republic they usually pour 3 fingers of head. But the measure line is also part way down the glass, the foam all above the line...
在 HN 中查看
ljm 2026/03/24 05:23
如果你愿意的话，你也可以只喝一品脱啤酒。

在西班牙，他们试图通过将啤酒倒入玻璃杯中（吉尼斯风格）并使其过度起泡来形成泡沫。所以它一半是平的，一半是满的。

You could get a pint of nothing but head as well, if you wanted.

In Spain they try to create the head by dumping the beer in the glass, Guinness style, and letting it excessively foam up. So it's half flat and half full.

True but a mlíko pour is a special request and usually cheaper.

ahartmetz 2026/03/24 03:23
德国也一样。在比利时，玻璃杯没有线条，也不会装满！唯一能阻止酒吧从你的啤酒中骗取你的啤酒的就是他们的声誉。说实话，我有时怀疑自己是否能得到我所付出的一切。
Same in Germany. In Belgium, the glasses don't have a line and they don't fill them to the brim neither! The only thing that prevents pubs from cheating you out of some of your beer is their reputation. And to be honest, I sometimes had doubts about getting all that I paid for.
在 HN 中查看
philipwhiuk 2026/03/24 00:52
有点儿。

吉尼斯啤酒杯正好是一品脱啤酒，因此吉尼斯啤酒杯意味着您喝到的啤酒还不到一品脱。

这是可以容忍/预期的，因此事实上是正确的，但在法律上可能不是。

Kind of.

Guinness glasses are exactly a pint, so the Guinness head means you're getting less than a pint of actual beer.

This is tolerated/expected and so de facto correct but de jure perhaps not.

在 HN 中查看
lucideer 2026/03/24 02:31
> 吉尼斯啤酒头意味着您喝到的啤酒还不到一品脱

我讨厌迂腐，但品脱是有体积的，你仍然会得到一品脱，与密度无关。另外，氮气头不会消散，因此永远不会出现间隙。

我现在很好奇氮气头的密度是否比二氧化碳头的密度低......

> the Guinness head means you're getting less than a pint of actual beer

I hate to be pedantic but pint being volumetric, you're still getting a pint, independent of density. Also - a nitrogen head doesn't dissipate, so you never get a gap.

I'm now curious though whether a nitrogen head is less dense than a CO2 head...

It feels much denser, and I think it does dissipate... but slowly.

lucideer 2026/03/24 05:25
我确信它最终会消散，但我曾在婚礼上收集过那些被遗忘而未动过的品脱酒——这确实是一种非常非常缓慢的消散。
I'm sure it dissipates eventually but I've worked at weddings collecting pints that were forgotten about untouched - it really is a very very slow dissipation.
在 HN 中查看
petesergeant 2026/03/24 00:50
这里唯一明智的方法是品脱到线的眼镜。我不希望我的杯子装满到最上面，否则我会把它洒出来，我希望它装到品脱线。遗憾的是，在英国，最多 5% 的品脱啤酒中允许含有泡沫，但如果我提出要求，我希望英国任何明智的调酒师都会给我加满品脱啤酒，并对此表示歉意。
The only sensible approach here is pint-to-line glasses. I don't want my glass filled to the very top where I'll spill it, I want it filled to the pint line. Sadly in the UK up to 5% of the pint is allowed to be foam, but I'd expect any sensible barman in the UK to top me up to the pint line if I asked, and be apologetic about it.
在 HN 中查看
BashiBazouk 2026/03/24 00:39
让我想起了在澳大利亚东海岸背包旅行的经历。我了解到福斯特斯 (Fosters) 是新南威尔士州北部唯一的啤酒店。每个地方都有自己喜欢的啤酒，但令人抓狂的是，他们都有自己的杯子。十艘，纵帆船。每个尺寸略有不同。我在霍巴特和一个住在旅馆里的人交了朋友，他在那里做研究，我认为他是一位生物学家。他带我去了他最喜欢的酒吧，因为他们提供帝国品脱啤酒。我认为这个网站的幕后黑手需要在澳大利亚进行一些认真的研究，因为他们至少可以将“了解你的玻璃”部分加倍......
Reminds me of backpacking up and down the east coast of Australia. I learned that Fosters is only northern New South Wales for beer. Every place had their own preferred beer, but maddeningly they all had their own glass. A tenner, a schooner. Each a slightly different size. I made friends with a guy in Hobart that was staying in the hostel as he was doing research there, I think he was a biologist. He took me to his favorite pub as they served imperial pints. I think who ever is behind this site needs to do some serious research in Australia as they could, at least, double the "know your glass" section...

mcbain 2026/03/24 01:23

不，我们当地人也不认识他们！

在 HN 中查看
hug 2026/03/24 06:24
作为一个深度参与酒店（阅读：啤酒）领域的人，这并不符合澳大利亚的现实：只有一个州（南澳大利亚）不同意主要标准尺寸：品脱为 470 毫升，大酒杯为 425 毫升，半品脱为 285 毫升，一瓶小马为 140 毫升。

有一半的口语：主要是锅或米迪。霍巴特会把半品脱称为十，因为它是 10 盎司，但当你要一壶或半品脱时，他们也知道你在说什么。

然后是南澳大利亚，它会为你提供 425 毫升的一品脱啤酒、285 毫升的大啤酒杯，除了特色精酿啤酒酒吧之外，没有人知道半品脱是什么，如果你想要一杯合适的品脱啤酒，你需要要一品脱英制啤酒。我从来没有在霍巴特看到过“帝国品脱”的广告——那里只是叫品脱。

资料来源：除了珀斯之外，我几乎在澳大利亚所有首府城市都有相当丰富的饮酒经验。

As someone heavily involved in the hospitality (read: beer) area, this doesn't really line up with reality in Australia: there's only one state (South Australia) that doesn't agree on the major standard sizes: Pints are 470ml, schooners are 425ml, a half pint is 285ml, and a pony is 140ml.

There's colloquialisms for a half: pot, or middy, mostly. Hobart will call a half pint a ten, because it's 10oz, but they also know what you're talking about when you ask for a pot or a half pint.

Then there's South Australia, which will serve you a pint at 425ml, a schooner at 285ml, no one there outside of specialty craft beer bars have any idea what a half pint is, and if you want a proper pint you need to ask for an imperial pint. I have never seen an 'imperial pint' advertised in Hobart - it's just called a pint there.

Source: I have pretty extensive drinking experience in pretty much all of the Australian capital cities, except Perth.

在 HN 中查看
November_Echo 2026/03/24 09:33
> 只有一个州（南澳大利亚）不同意主要标准尺寸：品脱为 470 毫升，大篷车为 425 毫升，半品脱为 285 毫升，一匹小马为 140 毫升。

> 资料来源：除了珀斯之外，我几乎在所有澳大利亚首府城市都有相当丰富的饮酒经验。

我不像以前那样喝酒了，所以这可能有点过时了，但在珀斯，“品脱”是 570 毫升。当你点一品脱啤酒时，有些地方会为你提供 470 毫升的大酒杯，这种情况很少见，但现在越来越少了。我们避开了那些地方。

> there's only one state (South Australia) that doesn't agree on the major standard sizes: Pints are 470ml, schooners are 425ml, a half pint is 285ml, and a pony is 140ml.

> Source: I have pretty extensive drinking experience in pretty much all of the Australian capital cities, except Perth.

I don't drink as much as I used to so this might be a little outdated, but in Perth "Pints" are 570ml. It was rare, but becoming less so, for some places to serve you a 470ml schooner when you ordered a pint. We avoided those places.

在 HN 中查看
hug 2026/03/24 09:54
...令人尴尬的是，我在原来的帖子中打错了字，现在编辑已经太晚了。品脱 是 东海岸到处都是 570 毫升（不是 470 毫升）——因此塔西半品脱通常被称为 10 品脱——因为它是 10 盎司，即半品脱 20 盎司/568 毫升。
...Embarrassingly, I have typo'd in my original post, and it's too late to edit. Pints are 570ml (not 470ml) everywhere on the East coast - hence why a half pint in Tassie is often called a ten - because it's 10oz, or half a 20oz/568ml pint.

hug 2026/03/24 08:26

在 HN 中查看
xnorswap 2026/03/24 00:29
最近，我在一家酒店的酒吧后面被一个新手给我倒了一品脱，她看起来很尴尬，因为酒含量大约是 40%，但值得赞扬的是，在我说什么之前，她就去叫了值班主管。

他解释说，倒好后，即使是剩下的头（修好后也有~3/4英寸）可能仍然会被很多顾客嘲笑。 “他们会问你是否愿意向他们收取一半的费用”等等。

虽然有一点回旋余地，但如果你少了一品脱太多，你很快就会听到这个消息。

I got poured a pint by a newbie behind the bar at a hotel recently and she looked embarrassed as it was about 40% head, but to her credit she went to fetch the shift supervisor before I said anything.

He explained after pouring it better that, even the remaining head (It had ~3/4 inch even after fixing it) might still be met by derision by many customers. "They'd be asking if you would be charging them for just for the half" etc.

There's a bit of leeway but you'll quickly hear about it if you short a pint too much.

在 HN 中查看
vojtapol 2026/03/24 00:40
是的。众所周知，美国人/加拿大人不能正确倒啤酒。如果您要倒比尔森啤酒或任何啤酒，至少 2 英寸的头部实际上是正确的，并且绝对是理想的。它在加拿大的倒酒方式（无头）对我来说几乎无法饮用。

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dggKezrSQxI

Yes. Americans/Canadians famously can't pour beer properly. If you are pouring a pilsner or really any lager, a head of at least 2 inches is actually correct and absolutely desirable. The way it's poured in Canada (no head) is borderline undrinkable to me.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dggKezrSQxI

大西洋两岸的偏好各不相同。这篇文章的另一条评论抱怨美国人倒啤酒的方式是错误的，因为他们_确实_用头倒啤酒。

> 同样在美国（可能是由于缺乏培训以及顾客不好意思抱怨）往往不会将其填满（因此甚至不会填满 16 英寸）。我见过2-3英寸的头，请他们加满。他们看着我，好像我刚刚侮辱了乔治·华盛顿

Preferences vary on both sides of the Atlantic. Another comment on this post complains that Americans pour beer wrong because they _do_ pour with a head.

> Also in the US (probably due to lack of training and the customer too embarrassed to complaining) tend not to fill it the brim (and so not even 16''). I've seen 2-3 inch heads and asked them to top it up. They look at me as if I've just insulted George Washington

masfuerte 2026/03/24 00:48
如果企业想要提供带杯头的饮料，他们应该使用侧面有刻度的玻璃杯，而不是需要装满的玻璃杯。 使用后一种类型的玻璃并包括一个头部只是在欺骗顾客。
If an establishment wants to serve drinks with a head they should use glasses with a mark on the side indicating the measure, rather than glasses which need to be brim full. Using the latter style of glass and including a head is just ripping off the customer.

bregma 2026/03/24 02:15
如何通过红色独奏布巴杯测量头部？

确实，没有一个有自尊心的加拿大人会用任何东西喝啤酒，除了直接从瓶子、罐子或小桶里拿出来的啤酒。

How do you measure the head through the red solo bubba cup?

Really, no self-respecting Canadian would drink beer out of anything except directly out of the bottle, can, or keg.

在 HN 中查看
consp 2026/03/24 00:50
我们把这种没有头的啤酒称为“死啤酒”。这里的测量大约是两个手指的宽度，如果你第一次做并且搞砸了，它就是两个手指的长度（第二部分当然是开玩笑）。
We call that kind of beer without any head "dead". Measurement here is about two fingers width, and if you do it the first time and screw up it is two fingers in length (second part is of course tounge-in-cheek).
在 HN 中查看
skipants 2026/03/24 01:06
作为一个加拿大人：很难同意。没有泡沫的啤酒很恶心。
As a Canadian: hard agree. Beer without head is gross.
在 HN 中查看
mattmanser 2026/03/24 02:52
你应该尝尝荷兰菜！

他们认为大啤酒头意味着啤酒质量好，你喝到的每一品脱啤酒都含有 80% 的啤酒啤酒头。

You should try the Dutch!

They think a big head means a good lager and every pint you get is 80% head.

dismalaf 2026/03/24 00:37
正确的？ 只需将其加满，让一些泡沫从侧面流下...如果放置时间太长，小桶中总会形成泡沫，因此无论如何您都需要放出一些泡沫。
Right? Just top it up, let some of the foam cascade over the side... Foam always forms in the keg if it sits for too long so you need to let some of it out anyway.

ch_123 2026/03/24 00:31
在英国和爱尔兰，一品脱为 20 盎司。 （相当于19美盎司多一点），所以我总觉得16盎司被骗了。美国的“品脱”眼镜。
In the UK and Ireland, a pint is 20 oz. (equivalent to just over 19 US ounces), so I always feel cheated by 16 oz. "pint" glasses in the US.
在 HN 中查看
lbourdages 2026/03/24 01:34
加拿大也是如此 [1]，但我经常看到这里以“美品脱”出售啤酒。我认为他们这样做是为了宣传更便宜的价格（金额较小）。有些地方会以盎司为单位写出玻璃杯的尺寸，但有些地方不会。

这肯定是我最讨厌的事情之一。

[1]： https://ised-isde.canada.ca/site/measurement-canada/en/buyin...

It is the same in Canada [1] yet I frequently see beer sold in "US pints" over here. I assume they do it so they can advertise cheaper prices (the amount being smaller). Some places will write the glass size in ounces, but some won't.

It is one of my pet peeves for sure.

[1]: https://ised-isde.canada.ca/site/measurement-canada/en/buyin...

在 HN 中查看
neuronflux 2026/03/24 04:40
也是加拿大人。我不经常在菜单上看到“品脱”，通常是“16盎司”之类的东西。显然，餐馆老板和酒吧老板都很遵守法律。不过当我在菜单上看到“20盎司”时我很高兴！

我有点理解这个逻辑，不提供 20 盎司并说“品脱”。顾客可能会避开某个地方，因为他们的“品脱啤酒更贵”，而实际上那个地方还为他们提供 4 盎司的额外啤酒。有点像经典的 1/3 磅芝士汉堡“更小”[1]。

令人烦恼的是，我确实发现服务员经常将他们的大容量啤酒称为“品脱”，无论菜单上写什么。

[1]： https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Third-pound_burger#Marketing_f...

Also Canadian. I don't often see "pint" on the menu, usually something like "16oz." Evidently restaurateurs and bar owners are wise to the law. Though I am pleased when I see "20oz" on the menu!

I kind of understand the logic by not serving 20oz and saying "pint". Customers might avoid a place because their "pints are more expensive", when in reality that place is also serving them 4oz of extra beer. A bit like the classic 1/3 lb cheeseburger being "smaller"[1].

Annoyingly, I do find that servers will often refer to their larger size beer as "pint" regardless of whatever the menu says.

[1]: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Third-pound_burger#Marketing_f...

在 HN 中查看
michael1999 2026/03/24 05:27
我有一个朋友，他曾经把酒吧上的重量和测量称为“品脱”。 这是非法的，但主要是通过投诉来执行的。
I have a buddy who used to call Weights and Measures on bars that passed off US customary pints as "pints". It is illegal, but enforced largely by complaint.
在 HN 中查看
vlod 2026/03/24 00:43
同样在美国（可能是由于缺乏培训以及顾客不好意思抱怨）往往不会将其填满（因此甚至不会填满 16 英寸）。我见过2-3英寸的头，请他们加满。他们看着我，就好像我刚刚侮辱了乔治·华盛顿一样。
Also in the US (probably due to lack of training and the customer too embarrassed to complaining) tend not to fill it the brim (and so not even 16''). I've seen 2-3 inch heads and asked them to top it up. They look at me as if I've just insulted George Washington.

mcjiggerlog 2026/03/24 00:51

Well, depending on the type of beer, that's intentional. It's not always the faux-pas that it would be to do this when serving cask ale in the UK.

bardak 2026/03/24 01:00
但通常在这种情况下，他们会使用玻璃器皿，玻璃上有 20 盎司的线，并为头部留有空间。
But usually when that is the case they will use glassware that has a 20oz line on the glass with room for the head.
在 HN 中查看
consp 2026/03/24 00:47
荷兰的一品脱通常是 500 毫升。在极少数情况下，但只有在真正的酒吧（而不是大众市场的“爱尔兰”酒吧）中，您才能喝到真正的品脱。所以在这种情况下你被骗了大约 68 毫升。与美国相比，您会多获得几毫升。
A pint in the Netherlands usually is 500ml. In very rare cases, but only in real pubs (not mass market "Irish" pubs) you get an actual pint. So you are cheated out of about ~68ml in that case. Vs the US you get a few ml more.
在 HN 中查看
amiga386 2026/03/24 01:31
据我所知，荷兰酒吧通常出售：

- 200ml“小笛”

- 250 毫升“pintje”（小品脱），通常装在“vaasje”（花瓶，锥形啤酒杯）中出售。这是典型的啤酒量度： https://nl.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pintje “所订购的啤酒杯容量通常为 25 厘升”

他们还出售 300 毫升标准瓶装啤酒和 330 毫升标准罐装啤酒： https://nl.wikipedia.org/wiki/Standaardglas

我不知道500毫升在荷兰是常见的。这在德国很常见，他们也卖 1 升的 方面

As far as I knew, Netherlands pubs typically sold:

- 200ml "fluitje" (little flute)

- 250ml "pintje" (little pint), often sold in a "vaasje" (vase, a tapered beer glass). This is the typical beer measure: https://nl.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pintje "Het bestelde glas pils heeft doorgaans een inhoud van 25 cl"

They also sell standard bottled beer in 300ml and standard cans in 330ml: https://nl.wikipedia.org/wiki/Standaardglas

I was not aware that 500ml was usual for the Netherlands. It is usual in, say, Germany, where they also sell the 1 litre Maß

在 HN 中查看
ahartmetz 2026/03/24 03:30
Maß 只存在于巴伐利亚和具有强烈巴伐利亚主题的地方，而在德国任何地方几乎不存在瓶装或罐装啤酒。 Faxe（丹麦语）销售 1 升罐装，一些捷克品牌销售或曾经销售 1.5 升塑料瓶 - 仅此而已。下一个常见尺寸是 5 升迷你桶。
The Maß is only a thing in Bavaria and strongly Bavarian-themed places, and almost nonexistent for bottles or cans anywhere in Germany. Faxe (which is Danish) sells one liter cans and some Czech brands sell or used to sell 1.5 liter plastic bottles - that's about it. The next common size is 5 liter mini kegs.

benhurmarcel 2026/03/24 06:48
在法国它是 500mL，实际上称为一品脱 (pint)
In France it’s 500mL and it’s actually called a pint
在 HN 中查看
ch_123 2026/03/24 01:00
标准美国品脱约为 473 毫升，因此美国品脱比英制品脱少约 95 毫升。
A standard US pint is about 473ml so a US pint is ~95ml less than an imperial pint.
在 HN 中查看
saalweachter 2026/03/24 06:08
19 世纪，在他们疯狂的同时（将英担重新定义为 8 英石或 112 磅），英国人将品脱重新定义为 20 盎司。

从此以后，全世界再也没有一品脱是一磅的地方了。

（美国以葡萄酒加仑为标准，因此一品脱现在和过去都是 1.04 磅。）

In the 19th century, at the same time they went stone-mad (and redefined the hundredweight as 8 stone or 112lb), the British redefined the pint as 20 oz.

After this point, there was no where the whole world round where a pint was a pound.

(The US standardized on the wine gallon, so a US pint is and was 1.04 lbs.)

在 HN 中查看
thrance 2026/03/24 01:29

Same thing here in France. Except I've never seen any "real" pints here, it's always 50cl.
在 HN 中查看
loeg 2026/03/24 00:41
在美国，提供 16 盎司或 20 盎司的选择并不完全罕见。 但我在“快速休闲”餐厅看到的更多，而不是酒吧或更高档的餐厅。
It's not completely uncommon to be offered 16 oz or 20 oz as options in the US. But I see it more at "fast casual" restaurants than bars or more upscale restaurants.
在 HN 中查看
cbm-vic-20 2026/03/24 01:23
当然，“盎司”的含义可能有所不同。 根据维基百科，“英国法律将英制液量盎司定义为 28.4130625 毫升，而美国习惯液量盎司正好为 29.5735295625 mL，美国食品标签液量盎司为 30 mL。”
And of course, what an "ounce" means may vary. According to Wikipedia "An imperial fluid ounce is defined in British law as exactly 28.4130625 millilitres, while a US customary fluid ounce is exactly 29.5735295625 mL, and a US food labelling fluid ounce is 30 mL."

eduction 2026/03/24 01:15
当我们点威士忌时，你们的酒吧请回报我们。据报道，亨特·S·汤普森 (Hunter S Thompson) 在大西洋彼岸的一家酒吧里打趣道：“这是什么，样品？”
Your pubs kindly return the favor when we order whiskey. As Hunter S Thompson is reported to have quipped in a bar your side of the Atlantic: "What is this, a sample?"
在 HN 中查看
BoxOfRain 2026/03/24 01:20
这很公平，无法与那个人争论。

就我个人而言，我希望我们使用皇家海军用来供应朗姆酒的半鳃。这是英国一品脱的 1/8，即 71 毫升，朗姆酒含量至少为 54%！

分数鳃是英国公制之前的射击测量方法，但它们仍然相当小气。英格兰为 1/6 鳃，苏格兰为 1/5 或 1/4 鳃，北爱尔兰为 1/4 鳃。

That's fair, can't argue with that one.

Personally I'd have us use what the Royal Navy used to serve its rum ration in, the half-gill. This is 1/8 of a British pint or 71 millilitres, and the rum would have been a minimum of 54%!

Fractional gills were the pre-metric shot measure in the UK, but they were still pretty stingy. 1/6 gill in England, 1/5 or 1/4 gill in Scotland, and 1/4 gill in Northern Ireland.

dfawcus 2026/03/24 01:15
英国和美国的液量盎司不同也无济于事。

英国的品脱是 568 毫升，美国的品脱显然是 473 毫升。

The volume of UK and US fluid ounces being different also doesn't help.

The UK pint is 568ml, apparently a US pint is 473 ml.

dghf 2026/03/24 00:39
这就是为什么当美国人声称使用英制单位时我会感到愤怒。如果他们这样做了，他们的品脱就会是正确的尺寸。
This is why I get agitated when Americans claim to use imperial units. If they did, their pints would be the correct size.
在 HN 中查看
cjs_ac 2026/03/24 00:47
美国人并不声称使用英制度量衡；他们使用习惯度量衡，在英国根据 1824 年度量衡法创建英制单位之前也使用过这种度量衡。
Americans don't claim to use imperial weights and measures; they use customary weights and measures, which were also used in the UK prior to the creation of imperial units with the Weights and Measures Act 1824.
在 HN 中查看
kube-system 2026/03/24 00:59
在美国有很多人不知道其中的区别，这种错误相当常见。
There are many people in America who do not know the difference, the mistake is fairly common.
在 HN 中查看
bee_rider 2026/03/24 02:02
此时它们只是美国单位，对吗？因为英国已经升级了。
At this point they are just American units, right? Since the UK has upgraded already.
在 HN 中查看
cjs_ac 2026/03/24 02:13
美国习惯单位的起源是英国，即使美国、利比里亚和缅甸是最后仍在使用它的国家。英国几乎完全采用了公制（码和英里仍然用于测量道路距离，品脱仍然用于牛奶和啤酒，上届政府做出了一个奇怪的决定，允许品脱葡萄酒，但没有生产商使用品脱，因为他们无法获得瓶子），但这些单位系统除了是否在任何地方使用之外还有其他身份。

编辑/更正：牛奶以 568 毫升的倍数出售，因此虽然数量为品脱，但测量单位为公制。

The origin of US Customary units is British, even if the US, Liberia and Myanmar are the last countries still using it. The UK has almost entirely adopted metric (yards and miles are still used for measuring distances on roads and pints are still used for milk and beer, and the last government made the eccentric decision to permit pints for wine, which no producer used because they couldn't get the bottles), but these systems of units have identities beyond whether or not they're in use anywhere.

EDIT/CORRECTION: Milk is sold in multiples of 568 mL, so while the quantities are pints, the measurement is metric.

在 HN 中查看
OJFord 2026/03/24 07:51
> 编辑/更正：牛奶以 568 毫升的倍数出售，因此虽然数量为品脱，但测量单位为公制。

您想通过这种方式做出什么区别？ 1 品脱 是 568毫升。

如果你指的是标签或其他东西，不，它们被标记为 1/2/4 品脱。通常也带有升标记。您还可以购买公制尺寸的瓶子，即在超市货架上，您经常会看到一个品牌的 2 品脱瓶和另一个品牌稍小的 1 升瓶。

超市的价格标签将以英镑/升为单位，无论瓶子是否品脱，如果这就是您的意思？

> EDIT/CORRECTION: Milk is sold in multiples of 568 mL, so while the quantities are pints, the measurement is metric.

What distinction do you intend to make by that? 1 pint is 568ml.

If you mean in labelling or something, no, they're marked 1/2/4 pints. Usually also with litre markings. You can also get metric sized bottles, i.e. on the supermarket shelf you'll often see one brand's 2 pint bottles next to another's slightly smaller 1l bottles.

The supermarket price labelling will be in £/litre, regardless of whether the bottle's pints or not, if that's what you mean?

在 HN 中查看
ascorbic 2026/03/24 02:13
啤酒和苹果酒是英国唯一合法不按公制体积销售的饮料。必须以 2/3、1/2 或 1/3 品脱的量供应。其他所有饮料都必须使用公制。
Beer and cider are the only drinks that are legally not sold by metric volume in the UK. They have to be served by the pint, 2/3, 1/2 or 1/3. Every other drink has to use metric.
在 HN 中查看
dghf 2026/03/24 04:09
但这仅仅意味着数量必须以公制单位表示，可能除了英制之外，对吗？例如。目前，我的冰箱里有一盒牛奶，上面标着“2272 毫升 4 品脱”。
But that just means the quantity has to be expressed in metric units, possibly in addition to imperial, correct? E.g. I currently have a carton of milk in my fridge that’s labelled “2272ml 4 pints”.
在 HN 中查看
rafram 2026/03/24 02:35
并不真地。英国在日常生活中使用单位的大多数事物（道路、烹饪、饮料尺寸、体重、公用事业、土地面积...）都使用英制单位，尽管理论上它们会转换为公制。加拿大也类似。
Not really. The UK uses imperial units for most of the things you use units for in daily life (roads, cooking, drink sizes, body weight, utilities, land area...), even though they theoretically converted to metric. Canada is similar.
在 HN 中查看
lmm 2026/03/24 10:24
> 英国在日常生活中使用单位的大多数事物都使用英制单位（道路、烹饪、饮料尺寸、体重、公用事业、土地面积...）

并不真地。老年人可能会用有趣的旧温度/措施做饭，并用石头称重，但它正在消失，当代烹饪书和健身文化都是公制的。我确实从未见过非公制的公用事业账单（即使它是稍微奇怪的公制，例如千瓦时或立方米天然气）。

> The UK uses imperial units for most of the things you use units for in daily life (roads, cooking, drink sizes, body weight, utilities, land area...)

Not really. Old people might cook with funny old temperatures/measures and weigh themselves in stones, but it's fading out, contemporary cookbooks and gym culture are all metric. I've literally never seen a utility bill in anything other than metric (even if it's slightly weird metric like kWh or cubic metres of gas).

在 HN 中查看
mrwh 2026/03/24 04:01
_人类_体重。我从小到大都用公斤来衡量除了人之外的一切，我猜这相当于它自己完全特殊的尺度，石头，自从我在英国以外遇到的人没有听说过。
_Human_ body weight. I grew up measuring everything in kilos apart from people, which has I guess what amounts to its own wholly idiosyncratic scale, the stone, that no one I've since met outside of the UK has heard of.

OJFord 2026/03/24 08:01
我真的不知道为什么，它只有 14 磅，为什么美国/加拿大坚持使用非常大的磅数，而不是像其他国家那样将其分解？

不过，公斤对于人类体重来说似乎也越来越常见，我认为这主要是由健身应用程序和社区推动的。我怀疑现在学校里的孩子们是否已经习惯了石头；出生体重也许只有磅和盎司，但我相信即使是医学上的公制单位，也可以根据父母的熟悉程度进行转换。

I don't know why really, it's just 14lb, why does the US/Canada just stick with very large numbers of pounds instead of breaking it up as with others?

Kilograms seem more and more common for human weight too though, largely driven by fitness apps & communities I think. I doubt children in school today are accustomed to stone; only pounds and ounces for birth weight perhaps, but even that is metric medically and converted for the parents' familiarity these days I believe.

Symbiote 2026/03/24 00:37
至少在英国，法律要求在供应啤酒、苹果酒和葡萄酒时使用带有标记的玻璃器皿。 （或者使用带标记的量筒并将其倒入饮料器皿中，有时会在葡萄酒中这样做。）

至少在一些欧盟国家也是如此，尽管我没有在维基百科之外进行过检查： https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fill_line

At least in the UK it's a legal requirement to use marked glassware when serving beer, cider and wine. (Or to use a marked measuring cylinder and pour that into the drinkware, which is sometimes done for wine.)

It's also the law in at least some EU countries, although I haven't checked beyond Wikipedia: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fill_line

在 HN 中查看
BoxOfRain 2026/03/24 01:23
在真正的英国时尚中，生啤酒和苹果酒的需求量是品脱，而葡萄酒则以毫升为单位出售。
In true British fashion the requirements for draught beer and cider are in pints, while wine is sold in millilitres.

g_lined 2026/03/24 06:00
当然。你不会买一品脱葡萄酒。
Of course. You're not going to buy a pint of wine.
在 HN 中查看
OJFord 2026/03/24 08:10
三分之一品脱是189.3毫升，一大杯酒是175毫升。其中三分之二（2/9 品脱）几乎相当于一小杯葡萄酒，126.2 毫升与 125 毫升。如果他们愿意的话可以工作
A third of a pint is 189.3ml, a large glass of wine 175ml. Two thirds of that (2/9 of a pint) is almost bang on a small glass of wine, 126.2ml vs. 125ml. Could work if they wanted
在 HN 中查看
NelsonMinar 2026/03/24 06:40
They are serious about this in Germany.在圣诞市场上，您通常会买一个 Glühwein 马克杯，在参观时保留并装满饮料。即使是这些小纪念品上也标有填充线，毫无疑问是由一些非常严格的德国标准维护者监管的。
They are serious about this in Germany. At the Christmas markets it's common to get a single Glühwein mug that you keep and have filled with drinks as you visit. Even these little souvenir keepsakes have a fill line marked on them, no doubt policed by some very stern German standards keepers.

RGamma 2026/03/24 00:33
在德国，测量啤酒是一件很严肃的事情，以至于欧盟委员会不得不对一项规则变更发表评论，该规则据称禁止使用啤酒杯来发泡饮料（事实并非如此，但德国希望更加严格）： https://www.bayern3.de/bier-steinkrug-eu-richtlinie

https://germany.representation.ec.europa.eu/news/klarstellun...

Measuring beer is serious business in Germany to the point the EU commission had to comment on a rule change that supposedly forbade the usage of steins for foaming drinks (it didn't, but Germany wanted to be extra strict): https://www.bayern3.de/bier-steinkrug-eu-richtlinie

https://germany.representation.ec.europa.eu/news/klarstellun...

tveita 2026/03/24 00:45
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fill_line

使用贴错标签的容器销售饮料应向当地消费者保护机构举报欺诈行为。众包应用程序在这里似乎是错误的工具。

Selling drinks in mislabeled containers should warrant a fraud report to your local consumer protection agency. A crowdsourcing app seems like the wrong tool here.

在 HN 中查看
bombcar 2026/03/24 01:03
美国是一个不同的地方；虽然我会说在欧洲一切似乎都是正确的 在 这条线，而在美国我会说大多数倒酒都到了边缘（可能给我 更多的 比广告的多）。
The US is a different place; though I will say that in Europe everything seemed to be right on the line, whereas in the US I'd say that most pours were to the very brim (likely giving me more than advertised).
在 HN 中查看
luplex 2026/03/24 00:41
最重要的是，泡沫必须高于线。

关于用传统陶瓷啤酒杯盛装啤酒是否合法存在一些争论，顾客无法验证泡沫是否真的从啤酒线上开始。

据我了解，这在德国是合法的，但前提是有明显的标志，告知顾客他们有权将啤酒倒入有标记的标准玻璃杯中以检查数量。 资料来源（德语）： https://www.abendblatt.de/incoming/article402102835/wer-hat-...

1899 年，慕尼黑成立了一个协会，以打击倒酒欺诈行为。它被纳粹禁止，并于 1970 年重新组建。他们到处测量啤酒。这篇文章是它的精神继承者。 德语： https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Verein_gegen_betr%C3%BCgerisch...

and crucially, foam has to be above the line.

There is some back and forth around whether it's legal to serve beer in traditional ceramic steins, where customers can not verify that the foam really starts above the line.

As I understand, it is legal in Germany, but only if there is visible signage that informs customers about their right to pour their beer into a marked standard glass to check the amount. Source (German): https://www.abendblatt.de/incoming/article402102835/wer-hat-...

In 1899, an association was formed in Munich to combat fraudulent pouring. It was banned by the Nazis and re-formed in 1970. They went around and measured beers. This post is its spiritual successor. German: https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Verein_gegen_betr%C3%BCgerisch...

我自愿参加类似的酒吧活动。

I volunteer for the pub equivalent of this.

cesaref 2026/03/24 03:19
关于通过度量衡来检查一品脱是否是一品脱的问题，我记得我大学里一位朋友的父亲，他负责斯塔福德郡的度量衡。我认为他是副警长或类似的人，官方品脱是收藏的一部分。

这应该是在 80 年代末——我不知道它是否仍在使用，但我有一种感觉，法律不一定会继续发展，所以我想如果在法庭上受到质疑，可能需要采取官方措施。

On the subject of the weights and measures to check that a pint is a pint, I remember the father of a friend of mine at university who was responsible for the weights and measures for Staffordshire. I think he was the undersheriff or something like that, and that the official pint was part of the collection.

This would have been in the late 80s - i've no idea if it was still in use, but i've a feeling that the law hadn't necessarily moved on, so I guess the official measure could have been required if challenged in court.

joombaga 2026/03/24 06:03
我无法理解摘要中的这两个说法：

> 两品脱中有 1 品脱的交付量低于 14.4 盎司（低于 90%）

> 37.9% 明显短缺（低于声称数量的 90%）

这些不是相同的主张但不同的百分比吗？二分之一就是50%，50%小于37.9%

I'm having trouble understanding these 2 claims from the summary:

> 1 in 2 pints delivered less than 14.4 oz (below 90%)

> 37.9% were significantly short (under 90% of claimed volume)

Aren't those the same claim with different percentages? 1 in 2 is 50%, and 50% is less than 37.9%

credit_guy 2026/03/24 07:21
如果您比较美国和英国的酒精饮料供应量，那么您可以得出两个结论，具体取决于您认为饮酒是好是坏。如果你认为它不好，那么美国更好，因为美国的餐馆往往提供较小的尺寸。我就在这个营地里。相比之下，英国的餐馆要慷慨得多。与美国的酒杯相比，酒杯显得巨大。我想有些人喜欢这样，但我不确定这是否真的值得钦佩。
If you compare the US and the UK regarding the alcoholic beverage servings, then you can reach two conclusions, depending whether you think drinking alcohol is good or bad. If you think it's bad, then the US is better, because the restaurants in the US tend to serve smaller sizes. I am in this camp. In the UK, by comparison, the restaurants are much more generous; wine glasses seem gargantuan compared to wine glasses in the US. I suppose some people like that, but I am not sure that's really something to admire.
在 HN 中查看
eternauta3k 2026/03/24 02:45
我不断回想起的一个想法是我们如何定义长度、重量和体积单位的巨大后果。产品通常以整数出售（1 升、0.5 升、1 公斤等）。如果你能回到过去并摆弄例如根据一品脱的定义，你会发现减少其体积会导致人们喝得更少，直到它小到人们只需点两品脱。
One thought I keep coming back to is the immense consequences of how our units of length, weight and volume were defined. Products are often sold with round numbers (1 liter, 0.5 liter, 1 kg, etc). If you could go back in time and fiddle with e.g. the definition of a pint, you'd see that reducing its volume would lead to people drinking less, until it's so small that people just order two pints.
在 HN 中查看
stuartaxelowen 2026/03/24 08:24
在台湾，喝啤酒的标准方式是喝大瓶啤酒，但每人分享一小杯。这样很容易让人不喝太多，并促进社交“让我给你倒酒”的行为。 （让不喝太多变得更难）
In Taiwan the standard way to drink beer is to get it in large bottles, but shared with a small glass per person. Easy to not drink much that way, and promotes the social “let me fill your glass” behavior. (making it harder to not drink much)

scoofy 2026/03/24 04:05

I discovered long ago that you might as well order a can in the states because you'll pay less and get effectively the same amount of beer.
在 HN 中查看
chaboud 2026/03/24 02:03
我开玩笑说，16 盎司美国品脱是一个长期的公制方案，旨在推动采用 500 毫升（约 16.9 盎司）作为衡量标准，这是一种巴甫洛夫机制，旨在欺骗喝啤酒的美国人，让他们相信公制系统实际上更好，因为它会产生更多的啤酒。 笑话就在他们身上。 我们都是12盎司罐装的！ 33厘升？ 噗……

德国人用 Kölsch Stange 来解决这个问题，这是一种 200 毫升的玻璃杯，它很容易消失，所以它会保持冰冷，你只需从 Kranz 那里再买一杯即可。

I've joked that the 16oz US pint was a long-play metric-system scheme to drive adoption of 500ml (~16.9oz) as a measure, a Pavlovian mechanic to trick beer-drinking Americans that the metric system is actually better because it results in more beer. The joke's on them. We're all about 12oz cans! 33cl? pfft...

Germans have it nailed down with the Kölsch Stange, a 200ml glass that so readily disappears that it stays cold and you just get another from the Kranz.

no one drinks it that way, except for those who drive. and believe me, for half a litre in half an hour one yields an acceptable beer temperature

zabzonk 2026/03/24 02:19

froh 2026/03/24 02:27
玻璃上有一个校准标记，液体必须填充到该线，顶部的泡沫是额外的。 因此，在非洲大陆，我们得到的更多，而不是更少。 所以从我们的立场来看，岛民似乎被敲诈了;-)

干杯

there is a calibration marker on the glass and the liquid has to be filled up to that line, the froth on top is extra. so on the continent we get more, not less. so from where we stand it looks rather like the islanders are ripped off ;-)

cheers

在 HN 中查看
zabzonk 2026/03/24 02:39
我不否认这一点，但我确实记得有人点了一壶啤酒，我被要求将其倒入几个没有明显标记的玻璃杯中，我试图最终得到大约 1/4 英寸的啤酒头，就像我在英国和我的荷兰朋友大喊“更多的啤酒头”或什至不礼貌的东西。

在荷兰我主要喝瓶装啤酒。我是约克郡人，至少是出生的。

I'm not denying this, but I do remember a jug of beer being ordered and me being asked to pour it into several glasses without noticeable markings, and me trying to end up with about a 1/4 inch of head as I would in the UK and my Dutch friends yelling "more head" or something even less than polite.

Mostly in the NL I drink bottled beer. And I'm a Yorkshire man, at least by birth.

在 HN 中查看
hencq 2026/03/24 03:23
是的，在荷兰，两根手指的宽度是常态。但如果超过这个数字，人们就会因为你给他们倒牛奶而感到不安。
Yeah, in the Netherlands 2 finger widths is the norm. But if it's more than that, people will get upset that you're pouring them milk.
在 HN 中查看
zabzonk 2026/03/24 04:09
我真的很喜欢在荷兰工作，但他们的食物和饮料我无法习惯。我在荷航做了一些咨询工作，自助餐厅提供的午餐让我反胃——人造肉、人造面包。我向与我一起工作的一位荷航员工抱怨了这一点 - “我们知道，我们知道，当然老板们从来不在这里吃饭”

另外，荷兰人对中餐的看法很奇怪（基本上是某种印尼菜——我喜欢印尼菜，但不是那个）。

不管怎样，你绝对没有要求对荷兰菜进行批评，但我很高兴我把它从心里吐出来了。所以，抱歉。

I really liked working in the NL, but their food and drink I could not get used to. I did a bit of consultancy at KLM, and what was offered for lunch in the cafeteria turned my stomach - artificial meat, artificial bread. I complained about this to one of the KLM staffers I was working with - "We know, We know, and of course the bosses never eat here"

Also, the Dutch idea of Chinese food is very strange (basically some sort of Indonesian thing - and I like Indonesian, but not that).

Anyway you in no way requested a critique of Dutch cuisine, but I'm glad I got it off my chest. So, apologies.

badgersnake 2026/03/24 00:28
自 1698 年起，我们就制定了法律，要求房东在英国提供标准化品脱啤酒。这并不是一个新问题。
We’ve had laws requiring landlords to serve standardised pints the UK since 1698. This is not a new problem.
在 HN 中查看
xnorswap 2026/03/24 00:32
1985 年是现在的年份： https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/1985/72/contents

这就是为什么所有酒吧都会有一个小牌匾，说明他们的酒杯尺寸。现在几乎都是25ml，但在很多地方35ml也很常见。您可以提供任意尺寸的酒，但不能在同一家酒吧内。

（编辑：更好的链接： https://www.gov.uk/weights-measures-and-packaging-the-law/sp... ）

1985 is the current one: https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/1985/72/contents

It's why all bars will have a little plaque saying what size their shots are. Almost always 25ml these days, but 35ml was common in many places. You're allowed to serve shots of either size but not in the same pub.

( edit: Better link: https://www.gov.uk/weights-measures-and-packaging-the-law/sp... )

在 HN 中查看
tialaramex 2026/03/24 01:10
他们达到 25/35 分的方式更加疯狂。在英国，法律规定烈酒的计量单位始终是六分之一鳃。整个单位已经过时了，但 1/6 比 25 毫升稍小一点。美好的。

然而在苏格兰，两种尺寸很常见，五分之一鳃（比英格兰稍大一些）和“nip”或四分之一鳃（大得多）。如果你习惯了点一些东西，但现在你得到的东西少了很多，你会很生气！因此，35毫升的选择是为苏格兰或爱尔兰的那种习惯于这些更大的措施而存在的选择，而不是试图保留鳃（这是一个其他人不需要的愚蠢单位）或激怒醉酒的人。

如果“做一个标志”的区别是允许许可经营场所逐渐转向更有利可图、更小的规模，我不会感到惊讶。也许你先把你在格拉斯哥拥有的地方换成25ml，如果当地人不启动你可以接下来尝试阿伯丁，否则几年后再尝试。

The way they got to the 25/ 35 split involves even more craziness. In England the law said spirits are always measured in sixths of a gill. This entire unit is obsolete, but 1/6 is a tiny bit less than 25ml. Fine.

However in Scotland two sizes were common, a fifth of a gill (slightly more generous than England) and a "nip" or quarter of a gill (a lot more generous). If you're used to ordering a "nip" of something and now you get a lot less you'd be very angry! So the 35ml option is there for the kind of Scottish or Irish establishment which would have been used to these larger measures rather than either try to keep the gill (which is a stupid unit nobody else needs) or anger drunk people.

I wouldn't be surprised if the "Make a sign" difference was to allow licensed premises to gradually shift to the more profitable, smaller, size. Maybe you change the place you own in Glasgow to 25ml first, and if the locals don't kick off you can try Aberdeen next, otherwise try again in a few years.

philipwhiuk 2026/03/24 00:53
我喜欢 CAMRA 的，它的玻璃边缘下方有品脱线。因为从技术上讲，酒头不算作品脱的一部分，而且对于法律上可接受的酒头含量到底是多少，存在很多争论（CAMRA 说 0%，酒吧协会提供 5%，酒吧本身提供他们认为您愿意支付的任何费用）。
I like the CAMRA ones which have the pint line below the rim of the glass. Because technically the head doesn't count as part of the pint and there's a lot of back-and-forth about what the legally acceptable amount of head really is (CAMRA say 0%, pub associations serve 5%, pubs themselves serve whatever they think you'll pay for).
在 HN 中查看
alexfoo 2026/03/24 06:14
> CAMRA 说 0%，酒吧协会服务 5%

这有点像苹果和橘子的比较。

CAMRA 会喜欢的真正的麦芽啤酒几乎没有任何泡沫，而普通啤酒可能需要耐心倒酒才能将其保持在 5% 的泡沫。

任何想要一杯无头吉尼斯黑啤酒的人都无法理解他们所点的饮料。

> CAMRA say 0%, pub associations serve 5%

That's a bit of an apples and oranges comparison.

A real ale that CAMRA would be happy with rarely has any head at all whereas a generic lager may need a patient pour to keep it to just 5% head.

Anyone asking for a Guinness with no head doesn't understand the drink they've ordered.

在 HN 中查看
wodenokoto 2026/03/24 00:30
> 79.3% 的浇注量不足（低于声称体积的 100%）

这是相当令人惊讶的。当我在咖啡馆和酒吧工作时，啤酒厂过去所做的就是在玻璃上画一个标记，并在其下方写上“0.5”（在市场上以升而非品脱出售），并且该标记高于[1]半升标记。请注意，它没有单位。

酒馆和酒吧以便宜或免费的价格获得啤酒，啤酒厂/经销商试图欺骗酒吧销售比他们预期更多的啤酒。

[1] 我们使用厨房的量杯进行了检查。也许有一个阴谋让厨房使用的食材太少。

> 79.3% of pours were short (under 100% of claimed volume)

That is quite surprising. What breweries used to do when I worked in cafes and bars was to paint a mark on the glass and under it write "0.5" (in market that sells in liters and not in pints) and this mark is above[1] the half liter mark. Note that it is without units.

Pubs and bars get glasses for cheap or free and the brewery/distributor tries to trick the bar into selling more beer than they expect.

[1] We checked using measure cups from the kitchen. Maybe there is a conspiracy to have kitchens use too little ingredients.

在 HN 中查看
calebelac 2026/03/24 00:36
感谢分享！很高兴看到这场酗酒悲剧背后有一些动力
Thanks for sharing! Glad to see there's some momentum behind this libatious tragedy
在 HN 中查看
tristor 2026/03/24 04:50
很高兴看到我不是唯一一个为此烦恼的人。 我去过超过 75 个国家、美国 43 个州、超过 2500 家啤酒厂，喝过超过 10,000 种独特的啤酒。 许多啤酒风格应该使用与标准品脱玻璃杯不同的玻璃杯形状来正确饮用，但短暂倒酒是一种流行现象，尤其是在美国和加拿大。 当我在酒吧喝一杯并看到他们使用真正的帝国诺尼克玻璃杯和倾倒线为 16 盎司/568 毫升时，我总是很感激。
Glad to see I'm not the only person that this bothers. I've been to more than 75 countries, to 43 US states, more than 2500 breweries, and I have drank more than 10k unique beers. Many beers styles should be correctly served in different glass shapes than a standard pint glass, but short pours are endemic, especially in the United States and in Canada. I always appreciate when I get a pint at a bar and see that they're using actual imperial nonic glasses with a pour line at 16oz/568ml.
在 HN 中查看
amonon 2026/03/24 00:55
看，我明白了。现在啤酒很贵。一直都是。但我觉得这些动作只见树木不见森林。

如果我们强制要求啤酒供应量，那么“短缺”的地方就会提高价格。更不用说支付检验服务所需的啤酒税了。没有人喜欢感觉自己得到的比付出的少，但解决方案是将您的业务转移到其他地方。

另外，你知道什么真正让我烦恼吗？当调酒师为我倒一品脱啤酒时，酒已经满了，没有泡沫。泡沫是清爽啤酒的乐趣之一。它增加了香气和期待。如果我想喝没有泡沫的东西，我会喝苏打水。在我内心深处（或者是胃，如果是胃？），我担心这就是这些争论的结果。

Look, I get it. Beer is expensive, these days. Always has been. But I feel like these movements miss the forest for trees.

If we mandate beer volume then places that are “shorting” you will just raise the prices. Not to mention the tax on beer that would be required to pay for the inspection service. No one likes feeling like they got less than they paid for, but there’s solution is to take your business elsewhere.

Also, you know what really annoys me? When a bartender pulls a pint for me, and it’s up to the brim with no foam. Foam is part of the joy of a crisp beer. It adds aroma and anticipation. If I wanted to drink something with no foam I’d drink a soda. And in my heart of hearts (or stomach if stomachs?) I fear that’s where these arguments lead.

在 HN 中查看
Johnny555 2026/03/24 01:03
没有人喜欢感觉自己得到的比付出的少，但如果没有监管，你怎么办？ 知道 除非你自己随身携带量杯，否则你得到的比你付出的要少？

如果做空的地方必须给你你所支付的价格时你必须提高价格，那就是错误的经济——你没有省钱，如果你想少喝啤酒以省钱，那就要求小一点的杯子。

No one likes feeling like they got less than they paid for, but without regulation, how do you know that you got less than you paid for unless you're going to carry around a measuring glass yourself?

If the places that were shorting you have to raise prices when they have to give you what you paid for, that's false economy -- you're not saving money, if you want to drink less beer to save money, ask for a smaller glass.

在 HN 中查看
goosejuice 2026/03/24 03:17
食品零售和食品服务之间存在明显的区别。这种规定会损害后来者的利益，它不属于。我们是否要对每个披萨、每个煎蛋卷、薯条的每一面都进行称重？我们不需要对饮食文化的每一个部分进行消毒。

任何在食品服务行业工作过很短时间的人都会熟悉收缩。平均棒材的收缩幅度可能超过 15%。短暂的倾倒可能无法抵消这一损失。边缘很薄。

该网站针对神经质饮酒者的解决方案吸引了： - 订购一罐或一瓶 - 购买零售商品并呆在家里 - 前往自浇点并按量付费。好处：您不必与任何人交谈。

否则收起秤并与调酒师交谈。您离开时很可能会得到大量免费啤酒。大多数小酒吧都会为您提供免费啤酒，帮助您找到您喜欢的啤酒。如果您一味追求餐饮服务的物有所值，那么您就错过了该行业的真正价值。

我们不要用秤来骚扰我们的调酒师，这是一项艰苦的工作。我在一家奶酪店的案例背后花了很多年的时间。秤有时间和地点。不是这样的。

There's a distinct difference between food retail and food service. This kind of regulation will harm the later, it does not belong. Are we going to weigh every pizza, every omelette, every side of fries too? We don't need to sterilize every single part of our food culture.

Anyone who has spent even a short amount of time in the food service business will be familiar with shrink. The average bar is probably seeing more than 15% shrinkage. The short pours are probably not offsetting that loss. Margins are thin.

Solutions for the neurotic drinker this website appeals to: - order a can or bottle - buy retail and stay home - go to a self pour joint and pay by volume. Bonus: you don't have to talk to anyone.

Otherwise put away the scale and talk to the bartender. Chances are you come away with plenty of free beer. Most small taprooms will help you find a beer you like by giving you free beer. If you're obsessing over getting what you paid for in food service, you're missing out on the true value of that industry.

Let's not harass our bartenders, a hell of a tough job, with scales. I spent years behind the case of a cut to order cheese shop. There's a time and place for scales. This is not it.

Bars are dying and are on thin margins so they have to do short pours, but if I just talk to a bartender, he'll give me plenty of free beer?
在 HN 中查看
goosejuice 2026/03/24 03:46
是的，一般来说，餐饮服务的利润微薄。附近的啤酒厂在最初几年可能不会盈利，如果成功的话，净利润率可能会稳定在 15% 左右。

如果您去啤酒吧或酒吧，调酒师的很大一部分作用就是帮助您找到您喜欢的啤酒。成功的酒吧和调酒师靠回头客而蓬勃发展。社区很重要。如果你真的坐在酒吧里与酒吧后面的人交谈，这一点就非常明显了。

Yes, generally food service operates on thin margins. A neighborhood brewery probably won't be profitable for the first few years, then if successful might stabilize around 15% net* profit margin.

If you go to a beer bar or a tap room, a large part of the role of a bartender is helping you find beer that you like. Successful bars and bartenders thrive from repeat customers. Community is important. This is very obvious if you actually sit down at bars and talk to the people behind them.

Forgeties79 2026/03/24 01:40
这是一件很小的事情，但是当一家以啤酒为特色的啤酒厂或餐厅写下“品脱”作为一个选项，并给我一个典型的美国啤酒杯（我相信是摇酒器）时，它让我很恼火。不 明确地 如果不超过 15 盎司，请写“品脱”。如果玻璃杯确实无法容纳一品脱，那么绝对不要这样做。
It’s such a small thing but when a brewery or restaurant featuring beers writes “pint” as an option and gives me a typical US beer glass (shaker I believe) it annoys the hell out of me. Don’t explicitly write “pint” if it isn’t at least north of 15oz. Definitely don’t if the glass literally can’t hold a pint.
在 HN 中查看
oldandboring 2026/03/24 01:17
我很喜欢阅读这个网站并欣赏它的热情和努力。

“一品脱的损失”基本上是收缩性通货膨胀。当酒吧的成本和/或管理费用上升时，他们必须采取一些削减开支和提高价格的措施。提高价格意味着要么以更高的价格出售相同数量的相同商品，要么以相同的价格出售更便宜的商品或更少的相同商品。大多数酒吧会选择后者，以避免涨价，因为涨价更容易引起投诉。当然，所有警告都适用——过多或过于频繁地降低份量或质量，你肯定会收到投诉，但“在合理范围内”，这是两害相权取其轻。

这就是为什么当我购买 8 美元 14 盎司摇摇瓶 IPA 时，我个人并不觉得自己被骗了 2 盎司。显然，该金条每盎司的成本为 0.57 美元。 一块 16 盎司的玻璃杯售价为 9.14 美元。他们不会因为使用了“品脱”这个词就免费欠我 2 盎司。 如果州政府再次开始实施品脱措施，酒吧只会从其招牌和菜单上删除“品脱”一词。

I enjoyed reading this site and appreciate the passion and the effort.

The "loss of the pint" is basically shrink-flation. When a bar's costs and/or overhead goes up, they must do some combination of cutting expenses and raising prices. Raising prices means either selling the same stuff in the same quantity for more, or selling cheaper stuff OR less of the same stuff at the same price. Most bars will opt for the latter options to avoid raising prices, because raising prices is more likely to create complaints. All caveats apply, of course -- drop portions or quality too much or too often and you'll get complaints for sure, but "within reason" it's the lesser of two evils.

This is why I personally don't feel like I'm being cheated out of 2oz when I buy a $8 14oz shaker pint of IPA. Clearly, the cost per oz at this bar is $0.57. A 16oz glass would cost $9.14. They don't owe me the 2oz for free just because they used the word "pint". If the state government started enforcing pint measures again, bars would just drop the word "pint" from their signs and menus.

你是来放松一下的。冷静一下。

You're there to chill. Just chill.

goosejuice 2026/03/24 01:34

As someone who worked in specialty foods for years, you get what you get. Flaws and all. That's part of the charm of this industry. This is especially true for small craft breweries. If you insist on accuracy then ask for a can/bottle list. If you insist on consistency then buy macro.
